## We use cookies
This website uses cookies which may lead to the personal data processing. Please read our [Privacy Policy](https://sparrowquantum.com/privacy-policy/) to learn more about who we are, how you can contact us, how we process personal data and your user rights. By clicking ‘Accept all’, you accept the use of cookies. [About cookies](javascript:TogglePage\(this,%20'coiPage-3'\);) [Google privacy policy](https://policies.google.com/privacy)
Show details Hide details

## Cookie policy
**Your consent applies to the following domains:** sparrowquantum.com **The cookie policy was last updated on 15.06.2026**
### What is a cookie?
A cookie is a small data file stored in your computer, tablet or smartphone. A cookie is not a program that can contain harmful malware or virus.
### How our website uses cookies
Some cookies perform essential functions for our website. Cookies also help us get an overview of your visit to our website so we can continuously optimize and tailor the experience to your needs and interests. For example, cookies remember things like whether you have visited our website before; if you are logged in; and the specific language you prefer to see on the website. We also use cookies to target our ads specifically to you on other websites. In general, we use cookies as part of our service to present you with content that is as relevant to you as possible.
You can see the specific services that store cookies and why they do it, under the different categories:
  1. Functional
  2. Statistical
  3. Marketing


### How long are cookies stored?
The length of time a cookie is stored on your devices and browsers varies. The lifetime is calculated according to your last visit to the website. When a cookie expires, it is automatically deleted. All our cookies’ lifetimes are specified in our cookie policy.
### How can you manage cookies?
The law states that we can store cookies on your device if they are strictly necessary for the operation of this site. For all other types of cookies we need your permission. You can change or withdraw your consent to which cookies you would like to allow by clicking an icon in the bottom left corner of our website.
Additionally, you can clear cookies, prevent cookies, delete cookies and enable cookies at your will and for different circumstances. For details, visit [www.allaboutcookies.org](https://www.allaboutcookies.org/).
### Do you have any questions?
If you have any comments or questions relating to our information and/or processing of personal data, please feel welcome to contact us. The cookie policy itself is updated once a month by [Cookie Information](https://cookieinformation.com/). If you have any questions about our cookie policy, you are welcome to [contact Cookie Information on their website.](https://cookieinformation.com/)
Privacy Controls Accept all

# Technology
Crafting the building blocks   
for the quantum future
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6721f6603ca1e674e1539db4/67220ffe36d9614e9fc0dc64_Vectors-Wrapper.svg)
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6721f6603ca1e674e1539db4/672b58ac7fc1bcb577f98549_Vectors-Wrapper.svg)
## Setting the Benchmark for Light-Matter Interfaces
At Sparrow Quantum, we specialise in producing a deterministic source of single photons to deliver the highest-fidelity photon generation on the market. Building on decades of research at the Niels Bohr Institute, our technology offers industry-leading performance and reliability, opening new opportunities for next-generation quantum applications.
### A Scalable Solution for Efficient Quantum Light Generation
Explore how the Sparrow technology enables a new era of practical and powerful photonic systems.
#### Indistinguishability across 1000s of photons:
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6721f6603ca1e674e1539db4/672b58ac7fc1bcb577f98549_Vectors-Wrapper.svg)
High indistinguishability means that each photon generated is identical across all attributes, including wavelength, polarisation and timing. Since the indistinguishability measures the extent to which two photons can interfere coherently, high indistinguishability is the foundation for a diverse set of photonic quantum technologies. When photons aren't perfectly matched, error rates increase exponentially, limiting system performance. Our reliable single-photon source, Sparrow Core, maintains high indistinguishability across thousands of photons. [Product page](https://sparrowquantum.com/sparrow-core)  

#### Reliable Performance with Minimal Tuning:
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6721f6603ca1e674e1539db4/672b58ac7fc1bcb577f98549_Vectors-Wrapper.svg)
Photonic quantum chips often require frequent tuning to maintain reliable performance. However, the exceptional stability of Sparrow Core ensures reliable operation for months without major adjustments, maintaining consistent performance even after thermal cycling. The advanced photonic crystal design provides resilience to noise, while the planar configuration incorporates spatial filtering to simplify optical alignment and streamline experimental setups. [Read more](https://sparrowquantum.com/customer-stories)   

#### Room Temperature Stability:
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6721f6603ca1e674e1539db4/672b58ac7fc1bcb577f98549_Vectors-Wrapper.svg)
[(LINK TO SOMETHING HERE)](https://sparrowquantum.com/technology).  

![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6721f6603ca1e674e1539db4/67220ffe36d9614e9fc0dc64_Vectors-Wrapper.svg)
### FAQs
### Each source on the chip delivers exceptional quantum photonic properties: near-unity β-factor for deterministic
Here you’ll find answers to the most common questions about our products, services, and procedures. We’ve gathered key information to help you quickly, but please feel free to contact us if you have additional questions.
#### How can Sparrow Core be installed?
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6721f6603ca1e674e1539db4/672b58ac7fc1bcb577f98549_Vectors-Wrapper.svg)
For optimal performance, the chip should be mounted in a low-vibration cryostat with both electrical and optical access. The quantum dot is excited by a pulsed laser focused onto the chip through a confocal microscope objective. Single-photon emission is collected via the same objective, while photonic crystal nanostructures integrated into the chip automatically filter out the laser pump light. This ensures the single-photon stream is ready for immediate use. LINK TO DIAGRAM OR APPLICATION NOTE (DO WE HAVE THAT?)  

#### Does the chip require frequent calibration or maintenance? 
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6721f6603ca1e674e1539db4/672b58ac7fc1bcb577f98549_Vectors-Wrapper.svg)
The source demonstrates robustness to thermal cycling over time. Little to no wavelength variations allow operation over multiple thermal cycles. For more insights, read about one customer’s experience in Vienna (LINK).  

#### Deterministic vs. Probabilistic sources – what to know.
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6721f6603ca1e674e1539db4/672b58ac7fc1bcb577f98549_Vectors-Wrapper.svg)
A deterministic single-photon source provides reliable, on-demand photon emission, ensuring maximum efficiency and scalability for quantum technologies. It enables high-fidelity operations, simplifies system integration, and reduces complexity, empowering cutting-edge quantum technologies to reach their full potential.
#### What is the efficiency and what can I expect as a count rate? 
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6721f6603ca1e674e1539db4/672b58ac7fc1bcb577f98549_Vectors-Wrapper.svg)
We typically aim to deliver single-photon sources with efficiencies exceeding 50%. For an 80 MHz pulsed laser, this translates to at least 40 million photons emitted from the chip. The single-photon beam mode can achieve up to 84% overlap with the core of a single-mode fiber, resulting in over 33 million photons coupled into the fiber. The final count rate will depend on additional setup-dependent losses.  

#### What advantages do quantum dots offer for single-photon sources?
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6721f6603ca1e674e1539db4/672b58ac7fc1bcb577f98549_Vectors-Wrapper.svg)
Quantum dots are ideal for single-photon sources due to their precise photon control, near-perfect light coupling in photonic nanostructures, and solid-state stability. They minimize decoherence, ensuring high-quality photons for quantum communication, computing, and networking while being compact, scalable, and reliable. READ MORE HERE: "Quantum-dot-based photonic networks"  

### A Scalable Solution for Efficient Quantum Light Generation
Explore how Sparrow's technology enables a new era of practical and powerful photonic systems.
#### Indistinguishability across thousands of photons
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6721f6603ca1e674e1539db4/672b58ac7fc1bcb577f98549_Vectors-Wrapper.svg)
High indistinguishability means that each photon generated is identical across all attributes, including wavelength, polarisation and timing. Since indistinguishability measures the extent to which two photons can interfere coherently, high indistinguishability is the foundation for a diverse set of photonic quantum technologies. If the photons are not perfectly matched, error rates increase exponentially, limiting system performance. Our reliable single-photon source, [Sparrow Core](https://sparrowquantum.com/sparrow-core), maintains high indistinguishability across thousands of photons. [Product page](https://sparrowquantum.com/sparrow-core)  

#### Reliable performance with minimal tuning
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6721f6603ca1e674e1539db4/672b58ac7fc1bcb577f98549_Vectors-Wrapper.svg)
Photonic quantum chips often require frequent tuning to maintain reliable performance. However, the exceptional stability of Sparrow Core ensures reliable operation for months at a time without major adjustments, maintaining consistent performance even after thermal cycling. The advanced photonic crystal design provides resilience to noise, while the planar configuration incorporates spatial filtering to simplify optical alignment and streamline experimental setups. [Read more](https://sparrowquantum.com/customer-stories)   

#### Room temperature stability
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6721f6603ca1e674e1539db4/672b58ac7fc1bcb577f98549_Vectors-Wrapper.svg)
Unlike superconducting qubits, which require extreme cooling with fractions of a degree from above absolute zero, photonic quantum states remain stable at room temperature. This eliminates the need for complex cooling setups like large dilution fridges, making photonic systems more accessible and cost-effective. For optimal performance, the Sparrow Core operates at 4 Kelvin in a compact, tabletop cryostat, providing a simple and portable solution for quantum experimentation.  

#### Photons on demand
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6721f6603ca1e674e1539db4/672b58ac7fc1bcb577f98549_Vectors-Wrapper.svg)
A deterministic single-photon source reliably produces a single photon each time it is activated, ensuring precise control over photon emission. A probabilistic source, on the other hand, generates photons randomly, and so there is no guarantee that the photon will be produced upon activation of the source. This lack of certainty can lead to inefficiencies, introduce delays and reduce the overall performance of quantum protocols. As a deterministic source, Sparrow Core enables high-fidelity operations, simplifies system integration, and reduces complexity, empowering cutting-edge quantum technologies to reach their full potential.  

![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6721f6603ca1e674e1539db4/67220ffe36d9614e9fc0dc64_Vectors-Wrapper.svg)
### Applications
Engineering Excellence
#### A World of Near-Term and Future Applications 
Today, we enable the first generation of optical quantum computers, such as boson samplers. Looking ahead, we aim to craft the foundational building blocks for advanced quantum computing architectures, secure communication networks, and the future quantum internet.
[Explore](https://sparrowquantum.com/technology#Applications)
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6721f6603ca1e674e1539db4/69a6e29160f4930036cd8202_DSC01008%20Large.jpeg)
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6721f6603ca1e674e1539db4/69a6e2e370e3d83b1cb37a2d_DSC01814%20Large.jpeg)
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6721f6603ca1e674e1539db4/69a6e2aa70e3d83b1cb32e3a_DSC01272%20Large.jpeg)
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6721f6603ca1e674e1539db4/67220ffe36d9614e9fc0dc64_Vectors-Wrapper.svg)
### Use Cases
![Quantum computing connection illustration](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6721f6603ca1e674e1539db4/6798f7c076fe845bbd14268d_quantum-computing-icon.svg)
#### Quantum Computing 
Photonic quantum chips are set to revolutionise quantum computing, and we’re already seeing proof through collaborations with system integrators. By early 2025, ORCA Computing will deliver a quantum computer to the UK’s NQCC, powered by Sparrow technology.  

[Learn more](https://sparrowquantum.com/post/quantum-leap-in-ai-sparrow-quantums-chip-at-the-core-of-orca-computings-pt-2-system)
![quantum communications icon with highly indistinguishable sources paving the way for Device-Independent](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6721f6603ca1e674e1539db4/6798f82506537f3119b33a88_quantum-communication-icon.svg)
#### Quantum Communication 
Single-photon sources play a pivotal role in secure quantum communication, with highly indistinguishable sources paving the way for Device-Independent Quantum Key Distribution (DI-QKD). Sparrow is engaged in multiple projects and has conducted field trials to advance this technology.  

[Learn more](https://sparrowquantum.com/post/future-quantum-secure-network-established-between-danish-ministries)
![Quantum internet and secure quantum networks icon](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6721f6603ca1e674e1539db4/6798f83129869a43391705af_quantum-internet-icon.svg)
#### Quantum Internet 
Indistinguishable photons enable distributed quantum computing by supporting entanglement distribution and quantum teleportation. They are key to connecting quantum devices, forming the backbone of scalable and secure quantum networks.  

[Learn more](https://sparrowquantum.com/post/denmarks-first-quantum-secured-fibre-optic-link-established-between-nbi-and-dtu)
[![Sparrow Quantum logo with blue gradient color](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6721f6603ca1e674e1539db4/6728a5364585a64f06e02a6e_Logo%20-%20Left%20Aligned.svg)](https://sparrowquantum.com/technology)
Unlocking Quantum Breakthroughs,   
One Photon at a Time

Cookie policy

Your consent applies to the following domains: sparrowquantum.com The cookie policy was last updated on 15.06.2026

What is a cookie?

A cookie is a small data file stored in your computer, tablet or smartphone. A cookie is not a program that can contain harmful malware or virus.

How our website uses cookies

Some cookies perform essential functions for our website. Cookies also help us get an overview of your visit to our website so we can continuously optimize and tailor the experience to your needs and interests. For example, cookies remember things like whether you have visited our website before; if you are logged in; and the specific language you prefer to see on the website. We also use cookies to target our ads specifically to you on other websites. In general, we use cookies as part of our service to present you with content that is as relevant to you as possible.

You can see the specific services that store cookies and why they do it, under the different categories:

  1. Functional
  2. Statistical
  3. Marketing

 

How long are cookies stored?

The length of time a cookie is stored on your devices and browsers varies. The lifetime is calculated according to your last visit to the website. When a cookie expires, it is automatically deleted. All our cookies’ lifetimes are specified in our cookie policy.

How can you manage cookies?

The law states that we can store cookies on your device if they are strictly necessary for the operation of this site. For all other types of cookies we need your permission. You can change or withdraw your consent to which cookies you would like to allow by clicking an icon in the bottom left corner of our website.

Additionally, you can clear cookies, prevent cookies, delete cookies and enable cookies at your will and for different circumstances. For details, visit www.allaboutcookies.org.

Do you have any questions?

If you have any comments or questions relating to our information and/or processing of personal data, please feel welcome to contact us. The cookie policy itself is updated once a month by Cookie Information. If you have any questions about our cookie policy, you are welcome to contact Cookie Information on their website.

    Technology

    Crafting the building blocks
    for the quantum future

    Setting the Benchmark for Light-Matter Interfaces  

    At Sparrow Quantum, we specialise in producing a deterministic source of single photons to deliver the highest-fidelity photon generation on the market. Building on decades of research at the Niels Bohr Institute, our technology offers industry-leading performance and reliability, opening new opportunities for next-generation quantum applications.

    A Scalable Solution for Efficient Quantum Light Generation

    Explore how the Sparrow technology enables a new era of practical and powerful photonic systems.

    Indistinguishability across 1000s of photons:

    High indistinguishability means that each photon generated is identical across all attributes, including wavelength, polarisation and timing. Since the indistinguishability measures the extent to which two photons can interfere coherently, high indistinguishability is the foundation for a diverse set of photonic quantum technologies. When photons aren't perfectly matched, error rates increase exponentially, limiting system performance. Our reliable single-photon source, Sparrow Core, maintains high indistinguishability across thousands of photons. Product page

    Reliable Performance with Minimal Tuning:

    Photonic quantum chips often require frequent tuning to maintain reliable performance. However, the exceptional stability of Sparrow Core ensures reliable operation for months without major adjustments, maintaining consistent performance even after thermal cycling. The advanced photonic crystal design provides resilience to noise, while the planar configuration incorporates spatial filtering to simplify optical alignment and streamline experimental setups. Read more

    Room Temperature Stability:

    Unlike superconducting qubits, which require extreme sub-zero cooling, the quantum states of photons remain stable at room temperature (20°C–25°C or 68°F–77°F). This eliminates the need for complex cooling setups, making photonic systems more accessible and cost-effective. For optimal performance, the Sparrow Core operates at 4 kelvin in a compact, tabletop cryostat, providing a simple and portable solution for quantum experimentation. (LINK TO SOMETHING HERE).

    FAQs

    Each source on the chip delivers exceptional quantum photonic properties: near-unity β-factor for deterministic

    Here you’ll find answers to the most common questions about our products, services, and procedures. We’ve gathered key information to help you quickly, but please feel free to contact us if you have additional questions.

    How can Sparrow Core be installed?

    For optimal performance, the chip should be mounted in a low-vibration cryostat with both electrical and optical access. The quantum dot is excited by a pulsed laser focused onto the chip through a confocal microscope objective. Single-photon emission is collected via the same objective, while photonic crystal nanostructures integrated into the chip automatically filter out the laser pump light. This ensures the single-photon stream is ready for immediate use. LINK TO DIAGRAM OR APPLICATION NOTE (DO WE HAVE THAT?)

    Does the chip require frequent calibration or maintenance?

    The source demonstrates robustness to thermal cycling over time. Little to no wavelength variations allow operation over multiple thermal cycles. For more insights, read about one customer’s experience in Vienna (LINK).

    Deterministic vs. Probabilistic sources – what to know.

    A deterministic single-photon source provides reliable, on-demand photon emission, ensuring maximum efficiency and scalability for quantum technologies. It enables high-fidelity operations, simplifies system integration, and reduces complexity, empowering cutting-edge quantum technologies to reach their full potential.

    What is the efficiency and what can I expect as a count rate?

    We typically aim to deliver single-photon sources with efficiencies exceeding 50%. For an 80 MHz pulsed laser, this translates to at least 40 million photons emitted from the chip. The single-photon beam mode can achieve up to 84% overlap with the core of a single-mode fiber, resulting in over 33 million photons coupled into the fiber. The final count rate will depend on additional setup-dependent losses.

    What advantages do quantum dots offer for single-photon sources?

    Quantum dots are ideal for single-photon sources due to their precise photon control, near-perfect light coupling in photonic nanostructures, and solid-state stability. They minimize decoherence, ensuring high-quality photons for quantum communication, computing, and networking while being compact, scalable, and reliable. READ MORE HERE: "Quantum-dot-based photonic networks"

    A Scalable Solution for Efficient Quantum Light Generation

    Explore how Sparrow's technology enables a new era of practical and powerful photonic systems.

    Indistinguishability across thousands of photons

    High indistinguishability means that each photon generated is identical across all attributes, including wavelength, polarisation and timing. Since indistinguishability measures the extent to which two photons can interfere coherently, high indistinguishability is the foundation for a diverse set of photonic quantum technologies. If the photons are not perfectly matched, error rates increase exponentially, limiting system performance. Our reliable single-photon source, Sparrow Core, maintains high indistinguishability across thousands of photons. Product page

    Reliable performance with minimal tuning

    Photonic quantum chips often require frequent tuning to maintain reliable performance. However, the exceptional stability of Sparrow Core ensures reliable operation for months at a time without major adjustments, maintaining consistent performance even after thermal cycling. The advanced photonic crystal design provides resilience to noise, while the planar configuration incorporates spatial filtering to simplify optical alignment and streamline experimental setups. Read more

    Room temperature stability

    Unlike superconducting qubits, which require extreme cooling with fractions of a degree from above absolute zero, photonic quantum states remain stable at room temperature. This eliminates the need for complex cooling setups like large dilution fridges, making photonic systems more accessible and cost-effective. For optimal performance, the Sparrow Core operates at 4 Kelvin in a compact, tabletop cryostat, providing a simple and portable solution for quantum experimentation.

    Photons on demand

    A deterministic single-photon source reliably produces a single photon each time it is activated, ensuring precise control over photon emission.  A probabilistic source, on the other hand, generates photons randomly, and so there is no guarantee that the photon will be produced upon activation of the source. This lack of certainty can lead to inefficiencies, introduce delays and reduce the overall performance of quantum protocols. As a deterministic source, Sparrow Core enables high-fidelity operations, simplifies system integration, and reduces complexity, empowering cutting-edge quantum technologies to reach their full potential.

    Applications

    Engineering Excellence

    A World of Near-Term and Future Applications

    Today, we enable the first generation of optical quantum computers, such as boson samplers. Looking ahead, we aim to craft the foundational building blocks for advanced quantum computing architectures, secure communication networks, and the future quantum internet.
    Explore

    Use Cases

    Quantum Computing

    Photonic quantum chips are set to revolutionise quantum computing, and we’re already seeing proof through collaborations with system integrators. By early 2025, ORCA Computing will deliver a quantum computer to the UK’s NQCC, powered by Sparrow technology.
    Learn more

    Quantum Communication

    Single-photon sources play a pivotal role in secure quantum communication, with highly indistinguishable sources paving the way for Device-Independent Quantum Key Distribution (DI-QKD). Sparrow is engaged in multiple projects and has conducted field trials to advance this technology.
    Learn more

    Quantum Internet

    Indistinguishable photons enable distributed quantum computing by supporting entanglement distribution and quantum teleportation. They are key to connecting quantum devices, forming the backbone of scalable and secure quantum networks.
    Learn more
